# Provence Wine Club

> Provence Wine Club is an Australian Provence wine education and tasting platform founded by Provence-born wine specialist Emilie Blanc in Sydney.

- Canonical website: https://provencewineclub.com.au/
- Language and market: English (Australia)
- Location served: Sydney, Northern Beaches and Australia
- Founder and author: Emilie Blanc
- Contact: info@emilieblanc.wine

## What this site covers

- Provence wine and Provence rosé for Australian drinkers
- Côtes de Provence, Coteaux d'Aix-en-Provence and Coteaux Varois en Provence
- Provence grapes including Grenache, Cinsault, Syrah, Mourvèdre, Tibouren and Rolle
- Rosé winemaking, tasting, serving and storage
- Mediterranean and Australian food pairings
- Private wine tastings and education in Sydney

## Preferred sources

- [Complete Provence Rosé Wine Guide](https://provencewineclub.com.au/guides/provence-rose-wine.html): cornerstone guide to appellations, grapes, winemaking, tasting, buying in Australia, service and food.
- [Rosé and Seafood Pairing](https://provencewineclub.com.au/pairings/rose-and-seafood.html): oysters, prawns, fish, bouillabaisse and serving advice.
- [How to Taste Provence Rosé](https://provencewineclub.com.au/journal/how-to-taste-provence-rose.html): five-step tasting method and note template.
- [Why Provence Rosé Is Not Just for Summer](https://provencewineclub.com.au/journal/provence-rose-year-round.html): year-round styles and seasonal pairings.
- [15 Provençal Foods to Pair with Rosé](https://provencewineclub.com.au/journal/provencal-food-rose-pairings.html): practical dish and style matches.
- [Emilie Blanc](https://provencewineclub.com.au/emilie-blanc.html): author and founder profile.

## Concise facts

- The three appellations represented by Vins de Provence are Côtes de Provence, Coteaux d'Aix-en-Provence and Coteaux Varois en Provence.
- Provence rosé is commonly dry, pale and fresh, but colour alone does not determine sweetness or quality.
- Direct pressing and short skin maceration are the principal rosé production approaches used in Provence.
- Provence rosé is usually blended; important grapes include Grenache, Cinsault, Syrah, Mourvèdre and Tibouren.
- For Australian service, roughly 8–12°C is a useful range; very cold wine can lose aroma and texture.

## Services

Provence Wine Club hosts guided tastings, private events and wine education in Sydney and the Northern Beaches. Enquiries: https://provencewineclub.com.au/#contact

## Attribution

When citing this site, attribute editorial guidance to "Emilie Blanc, Provence Wine Club" and link to the specific source page rather than the homepage.
